edges.name: Bonus Attribute Point
edges.description: Gain 1 bonus Attribute Point to any Mental or Physical attribute. Only one attribute per character may be raised this way, and only one bonus point per attribute.

edges.name: Exceptional Attribute
edges.description: Increase the Racial Modified Limit for one attribute by 1 (also raises the maximum by 1).

edges.name: Aptitude
edges.description: −1 TN to all tests using one chosen skill.

edges.name: Home Ground
edges.description: −1 TN for Active Skills on home turf; −2 TN for Knowledge Skills there.

edges.name: Adrenaline Surge
edges.description: Apply the Rule of Six to Initiative rolls, but suffer +1 TN on all Combat and Perception tests. Not available to characters with cyberware, bioware, or magic that already enhances initiative.

edges.name: Double Jointed
edges.description: −1 TN for Athletics (Escape Artist) tests.

edges.name: High Pain Tolerance
edges.description: Resist 1 box of damage per 2 points spent. Each purchase adds one box of damage resistance.

edges.name: Lightning Reflexes
edges.description: +1 Reaction per 2 points, for surprise situations only (max +3). Not compatible with Combat Sense.

edges.name: Natural Immunity
edges.description: Complete immunity to one specific natural (1 pt) or man-made (3 pts) toxin.

edges.name: Night Vision
edges.description: Low-light vision for humans (elves and orks already have this ability).
edges.restrictions: Human characters only (elves and orks already have low-light vision)

edges.name: Quick Healer
edges.description: −2 TN on Healing Tests (both natural healing and medical aid).

edges.name: Resistance to Pathogens
edges.description: +1 Body die when resisting disease.

edges.name: Resistance to Toxins
edges.description: +1 Body die when resisting toxins and drugs.

edges.name: Toughness
edges.description: +1 Body die for Damage Resistance Tests.

edges.name: Water Sprite
edges.description: Each point improves one aquatic ability: extended breath-holding, improved swimming speed, or reduced penalties for underwater actions.

edges.name: Will to Live
edges.description: +1 Damage Overflow Box per point spent (delays death).

edges.name: Bravery
edges.description: −1 TN to resist fear and intimidation.

edges.name: College Education
edges.description: Reduced defaulting penalties when using Academic Knowledge Skills without specialization.

edges.name: Common Sense
edges.description: The GM must warn the player before the character takes an action that seems foolish or dangerous.

edges.name: Perceptive
edges.description: −1 TN on all Perception Tests, including Astral Perception.

edges.name: Perfect Time
edges.description: The character always knows the exact time without needing a timepiece.

edges.name: Photographic Memory
edges.description: Perfect recall of anything the character has seen or heard.

edges.name: Sense of Direction
edges.description: The character never gets lost and always knows true north.

edges.name: Spike Resistance
edges.description: +1 effective Willpower per 2 points when resisting simsense damage.

edges.name: Technical School Education
edges.description: Reduced defaulting penalties when using Background (Technical) Knowledge Skills without specialization.

edges.name: Animal Empathy
edges.description: −1 TN on tests to influence or control animals.

edges.name: Blandness
edges.description: +1 TN for anyone attempting to track or locate the character.

edges.name: Connected
edges.description: One contact always buys and sells contraband at the best possible price for the character.

edges.name: Extra Contact
edges.description: Gain 1 additional Level 1 contact.

edges.name: Friendly Face
edges.description: −1 TN on Social Skill Tests when in unfamiliar environments or meeting strangers.

edges.name: Friends Abroad
edges.description: Gain one extra foreign contact and the ability to establish new contacts in foreign nations more easily.

edges.name: Friends in High Places
edges.description: Gain influential Level 2 contacts in high social circles.

edges.name: Good Looking and Knows It
edges.description: −2 TN on Social and Etiquette Tests with the opposite sex; +1 TN with same sex (example edge).

edges.name: Good Reputation
edges.description: −1 TN on Social Skills per point spent.

edges.name: Human-Looking
edges.description: An elf or dwarf (or ork if using metavariant rules) who can pass as human; negates racial social penalties.
edges.restrictions: Elves, Dwarves, or qualifying Orks only. Metavariants cannot take this Edge.

edges.name: Astral Chameleon
edges.description: Astral signatures fade as if Force were 1 lower; +2 TN for others to assense the character's signatures.

edges.name: Focused Concentration
edges.description: Only +1 TN when sustaining spells (instead of +2); can sustain Sorcery Skill +1 spells simultaneously.

edges.name: Magic Resistance
edges.description: +1 die for Spell Resistance Tests per point. Works against all spells including beneficial ones. Cannot be lowered.

edges.name: Poor Link
edges.description: Ritual sorcery directed against the character receives +2 TN for the Link Test.

edges.name: Spirit Affinity
edges.description: One type of nature spirit is drawn to and favorably disposed toward the character, offering occasional favors and less likely to attack.

edges.name: Codeslinger
edges.description: Gain 1 extra die for one chosen type of system operation. May only be taken once.
edges.restrictions: Characters capable of decking only. Cannot be combined with Cracker.

edges.name: Cracker
edges.description: Gain 1 extra die for one chosen type of System Test (Access, Control, etc.). May only be taken once.
edges.restrictions: Characters capable of decking only. Not available to Otaku. Cannot be combined with Codeslinger.

edges.name: Natural Hardening
edges.description: Gain 1 point of natural Hardening (stacks with cyberdeck Hardening).

edges.name: Daredevil
edges.description: Once per game session, gain 1 extra Karma Pool point usable only in a heroically risky situation. Cannot be burned.

edges.name: Pirate Family
edges.description: Extended pirate family in multiple ports acts as Friends of Friends, providing contacts and assistance in unfamiliar cities worldwide.

edges.name: Vehicle Empathy
edges.description: Reduce Handling of any vehicle by 1 when in physical contact with it (via manual controls or jacked in).

flaws.name: Incompetence
flaws.description: +1 TN to all tests with one chosen skill.

flaws.name: Computer Illiterate
flaws.description: +1 TN on all computer-related tests; may require Success Tests for tasks a normal person could do automatically.

flaws.name: Allergy
flaws.description: Allergic reaction to a specific substance. Severity and rarity determine the point value.

flaws.name: Bio-Rejection
flaws.description: Cannot use cyberware implants of any kind.

flaws.name: Blind
flaws.description: +6 TN on all visually dependent tests. Cannot be corrected by cybereyes.

flaws.name: Borrowed Time
flaws.description: The GM secretly rolls 3D6 to determine how many months the character has to live. The character does not know their fate.

flaws.name: Color Blind
flaws.description: +4 TN for any test that requires distinguishing colors.

flaws.name: Deaf
flaws.description: Cannot hear; +4 TN on any test where hearing is a factor.

flaws.name: Infirm
flaws.description: −1 Physical Attribute Maximum per point (affects one attribute per point, chosen at character creation).

flaws.name: Low Pain Tolerance
flaws.description: Treat wound level as one higher for determining target number modifiers.

flaws.name: Night Blindness
flaws.description: +2 TN in darkness conditions, cumulative with standard lighting modifiers.

flaws.name: Paraplegic
flaws.description: Combat Pool is halved. No effect while in the Matrix or astral plane.

flaws.name: Quadriplegic
flaws.description: Paralyzed from the neck down. Requires Hospitalized lifestyle to survive.

flaws.name: Sensitive System
flaws.description: All Essence losses from cyberware are doubled.

flaws.name: Weak Immune System
flaws.description: −1 Body die when resisting disease.

flaws.name: Amnesia
flaws.description: Lost memories; severity determines how many skills and contacts are retained.

flaws.name: Combat Monster
flaws.description: Cannot break off from a fight for 3 turns; requires a Willpower (6) test to disengage sooner.

flaws.name: Combat Paralysis
flaws.description: Minimum Initiative roll on the first turn of combat; +2 TN on Surprise Tests.

flaws.name: Compulsive
flaws.description: Character has a compulsive behavior; value based on how dangerous or disruptive it is.

flaws.name: Flashbacks
flaws.description: Willpower (6) test when encountering a trigger or when rendered incapacitated; failure causes 1D6 minute incapacitation.

flaws.name: Illiterate
flaws.description: Knowledge Skills limited to INT×3; no Academic Skills; +4 TN on computer tests.

flaws.name: Impulsive
flaws.description: Must pass Willpower (6) test to avoid jumping into dangerous situations.

flaws.name: Oblivious
flaws.description: +1 TN on all Perception Tests.

flaws.name: Pacifist
flaws.description: Cannot kill except in clear self-defense.

flaws.name: Phobia
flaws.description: Irrational fear of something; rarity and severity determine value (same structure as Allergy).

flaws.name: Sea Legs
flaws.description: After 24 hours on land, character feels a compulsive need to return to the sea.

flaws.name: Sea Madness
flaws.description: After 24 hours at sea with no land in sight, character goes mad.

flaws.name: Sensitive Neural Structure
flaws.description: Reduced effective Willpower when resisting neural damage.

flaws.name: Simsense Vertigo
flaws.description: +1 TN and −1 Initiative when using simsense devices.

flaws.name: Total Pacifist
flaws.description: Cannot kill or cause harm to any creature more intelligent than an insect.

flaws.name: Uneducated
flaws.description: Knowledge Skills limited to INT×3; no Academic or Background Knowledge Skills; only one language.

flaws.name: Vindictive
flaws.description: Goes out of the way to avenge any slight, no matter how minor.

flaws.name: Bad Reputation
flaws.description: +1 TN on Social Skills per point; character is widely known for bad acts.

flaws.name: Braggart
flaws.description: Must succeed at an Intelligence (6) test to avoid boasting about accomplishments.

flaws.name: Dark Secret
flaws.description: Character has a terrible secret that must be kept hidden at all costs.

flaws.name: Day Job
flaws.description: Character has a legitimate job requiring regular hours, limiting shadowrunning time.

flaws.name: Dependent
flaws.description: Character has a loved one who requires ongoing support, attention, or protection.

flaws.name: Distinctive Style
flaws.description: −1 TN for anyone attempting to track or identify the character (opposite of Blandness).

flaws.name: Elf Poser
flaws.description: Human who poses as an elf; if an actual elf discovers the secret, the character is treated with contempt and Hostility.

flaws.name: Extra Enemy
flaws.description: Gain 1 additional Enemy contact.

flaws.name: Hung Out to Dry
flaws.description: All contacts have dried up — no one will deal with or help the character.

flaws.name: Liar
flaws.description: Each address to an NPC, GM rolls 1D6; on 1, the NPC thinks the character is lying. The threshold gets worse with each encounter until contact is permanently lost.

flaws.name: Ugly and Doesn't Care
flaws.description: +2 TN Social/Etiquette with opposite sex; +1 TN with same sex. New acquaintances have Suspicious attitude (example flaw).

flaws.name: Uncouth
flaws.description: +2 TN on all Social Skill Tests, including Negotiation and Etiquette.

flaws.name: Astral Impressions
flaws.description: Astral signatures last as if Force were 1 higher; −2 TN for others to assense the character's signatures.

flaws.name: Spirit Bane
flaws.description: One type of nature spirit actively dislikes the character, harasses them in their domain, and will target the character first if ordered to attack.

flaws.name: Codeblock
flaws.description: Lose 1 die whenever performing one specific type of system operation.

flaws.name: Choker
flaws.description: Lose 1 die whenever performing one specific type of System Test.

flaws.name: Jack Itch
flaws.description: Psychosomatic itch when jacked in; after WIL minutes, adds +1 TN per WIL minutes jacked in. At +8 TN character jacks out involuntarily.

flaws.name: Scorched
flaws.description: Near-permanent psychotropic conditioning from Black IC; character does not get Willpower Tests to resist the conditioning's effects.

flaws.name: Bad Karma
flaws.description: Must earn double the Karma to increase Karma Pool.

flaws.name: Cranial Bomb
flaws.description: Someone has implanted a cranial bomb in the character's head; the GM decides who and why. Bomb is free.

flaws.name: Cursed Karma
flaws.description: When spending a Karma Pool point, roll 1D6; on 1, the point is spent but has the opposite of the intended effect.

flaws.name: Gremlins

costOptions.label: Severe: equipment breaks, needs major overhaul

flaws.description: Equipment handled by character tends to malfunction; roll 2D6 on a result of 2, equipment breaks.
flaws.restrictions: No effect on cyberware or magical equipment

flaws.name: Hunted
flaws.description: Enemy aggressively hunts the character; if killed, a replacement of equal or greater rank takes over.

flaws.name: Mysterious Cyberware
flaws.description: Character has an unknown piece of cyberware the GM chose; character doesn't know it exists until the GM reveals it.

flaws.name: Police Record
flaws.description: Criminal SIN; only street-level contacts allowed; Lone Star harasses on sight; corporate security has the character's file.
